strengths: Proven backend and distributed-systems expertise with experience in high-throughput microservices (e.g., Dunnhumby media handling millions of requests/second)
Recent hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling (e.g., MCP code generators, agentic workflows at re:cinq)
Strong engineering leadership in scalable systems, including event-driven architectures (e.g., GISCollective platform), and team scaling (e.g., Altom Consulting dev-ops infrastructure)
concerns: Limited direct fintech experience, with no clear evidence of credit systems, payment flows, or embedded finance work
No demonstrated experience with real-time inference systems or production ML feature engineering in regulated environments
Lacks explicit track record in a venture-backed scale-up (current role at re:cinq is too new to assess for this role)
response.justification: The candidate demonstrates strong backend and distributed-systems expertise, including recent work with AI-agent infrastructure, which aligns with the agentic AI architecture requirements. However, there is a notable gap in fintech-specific experience (e.g., credit systems, real-time inference) and regulated environment operations, which are critical for this role. The lack of a clear venture-backed scale-up track record also raises concerns about readiness for the executive demands of this position.
